About Mark — Clearances in Devon

Mark is the founder and owner of Clearances in Devon, a local family business based in Devon providing professional, reliable and eco-friendly clearance services across the whole of Devon.

A Local Family Business You Can Trust

Mark started Clearances in Devon with a simple aim — to provide honest, hardworking clearance services at fair prices, with no hidden fees and no fuss. As a local family business, Mark takes personal pride in every job, big or small.

When you contact Mark, you speak directly to Mark. There are no call centres, no subcontractors and no surprises. Mark and his team will arrive on time, work efficiently and leave your property clean and clear.

Licensed and Fully Compliant

Mark is a fully registered waste carrier, licensed by the Environment Agency. This means every item removed from your property is disposed of legally and responsibly. You will never be at risk of fly-tipping fines or illegal disposal when you use Mark’s services.

Eco-Friendly Disposal

Mark recycles and donates items wherever possible. Furniture, appliances and other usable items are passed on to local charities and community groups where they can do some good, rather than going straight to landfill.

Coverage Across Devon

Mark covers the whole of Devon, from Exeter and Plymouth to Barnstaple, Torquay, Tiverton and all towns and villages in between. No location is too remote.

Opening Hours

Mark is available Monday to Saturday, 7:00am to 5:00pm. Sunday is closed.

Get in Touch with Mark

Call Mark directly on 07867180703 or request a free quote online. Mark will get back to you promptly with a fair, no-obligation price.

Cookie Policy

This cookie policy explains how Clearances in Devon uses cookies on this website.

What Are Cookies?

Cookies are small text files placed on your device when you visit a website. They are widely used to make websites work correctly and to provide basic functionality.

How We Use Cookies

This website uses only essential cookies necessary for the site to function correctly. We do not use any tracking cookies, advertising cookies or third-party analytics cookies.

Third-Party Cookies

This website does not load any third-party scripts or services that place cookies on your device.

Managing Cookies

You can control and delete cookies through your browser settings. Please note that disabling cookies may affect the functionality of this website.

For more information about managing cookies, visit www.aboutcookies.org.

Changes to This Policy

This cookie policy may be updated from time to time. Any changes will be published on this page.

Keeping Cornwall Clean

Although Clearances in Devon is based in Devon and primarily serves the whole of Devon, Mark and his team are deeply committed to responsible waste disposal and doing their part to keep the South West clean.

Licensed and Responsible

Mark is a fully registered waste carrier, licensed by the Environment Agency. Every item collected during a clearance is handled responsibly — reused, recycled or disposed of at a licensed facility. Nothing is fly-tipped. Nothing is dumped illegally.

Why This Matters

Fly-tipping is a serious problem across Devon and Cornwall. It blights communities, harms wildlife and costs local councils millions of pounds every year to clear up. By choosing a licensed waste carrier like Mark, you are actively helping to reduce fly-tipping and protect the environment.

What You Can Do

If you need a clearance or rubbish removal service, always check that the person you hire is a licensed waste carrier. You can verify any waste carrier’s licence on the Environment Agency website.

Never hand your waste to an unlicensed operator. If waste removed from your property is fly-tipped, you could be held liable even if you did not do the tipping yourself.

body_content:

Reuse and Recycling Policy

At Clearances in Devon, Mark is committed to minimising the amount of waste sent to landfill. Wherever possible, items collected during clearances are reused, recycled or donated.

Reuse First

Mark’s first priority is always reuse. Furniture, appliances, clothing, books and other usable items are assessed during every clearance. Where items are in good condition, Mark will pass them on to local charities, community groups or individuals who can make use of them.

Recycling

Items that cannot be reused are sorted for recycling wherever possible. Metal, wood, cardboard, glass and plastics are separated and taken to licensed recycling facilities across Devon.

Responsible Disposal

Any items that cannot be reused or recycled are disposed of legally and responsibly at licensed waste facilities. Mark is a fully registered waste carrier, licensed by the Environment Agency, and complies fully with all waste disposal regulations.

Hazardous Waste

Mark is unable to collect or dispose of hazardous materials including asbestos, clinical waste, chemicals or paint. If you are unsure whether an item can be collected, please ask Mark before booking.

Our Commitment

Mark believes that responsible disposal is not just a legal obligation — it is the right thing to do. Every clearance carried out by Mark and his local family business is handled with care for both the customer and the environment.

If you have any questions about how we handle waste, please call Mark on 07867180703.
